body:
## Problem

A parent sub-orchestration can silently stop after a child PR reaches `SHIP` instead of reporting the child completion back to the parent and continuing to the next planned child task.

This was observed from #126 after the orchestration request in https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/issues/126#issuecomment-4369999173.

## Observed sequence

1. Parent issue #126 was asked to orchestrate the task split and create/stack individual child issues.
2. Child #25 (`agent-activity-labels`) completed through PR #134 and correctly reported back to #126.
3. Parent orchestration then created child #135 (`assign-agent-handle`) stacked on #134.
4. Child #135 produced PR #136.
5. PR #136 eventually reviewed as `SHIP`.
6. The final orchestrator run stopped with the normal PR-level terminal reason:

   ```text
   SOURCE_ACTION: review
   SOURCE_CONCLUSION: ship
   TARGET_KIND: pull_request
   TARGET_NUMBER: 136
   Handoff stop: review verdict is SHIP
   ```

   Run: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/actions/runs/25318313262

7. However, #126 never received a child-completed report for #135, and parent orchestration did not continue to the next child task.
8. #135's sub-orchestrator marker remained stuck as `state:running`:

   ```md
   <!-- sepo-sub-orchestrator parent:126 stage:assign-agent-handle state:running parent_round:3 -->
   ```

## Expected behavior

When a sub-orchestrator child PR reaches a terminal successful state (`review` + `SHIP`):

- mark the child sub-orchestrator marker as `done`;
- post a completion/progress report to the parent issue;
- dispatch the parent orchestrator so it can decide whether to create/run the next child task;
- avoid making the workflow appear stopped when only the child task finished.

## Suspected root cause

The terminal reporting path appears to fail to recognize the child issue marker as trusted when the child issue was created by the GitHub App.

Relevant flow:

- `reportTerminalToParent()` resolves the linked child issue from the PR body (`Closes #135`).
- It calls `trustedSubOrchestrationIssue()`.
- For Sepo-created child #135, the sub-orchestrator marker is in the issue body.
- `gh issue view 135 --json author` reports the author login as:

  ```json
  {"author":{"is_bot":true,"login":"app/sepo-agent-app"}}
  ```

- Other trusted comments often appear as `sepo-agent-app[bot]` / `sepo-agent-app`.
- `normalizeActorLogin()` currently strips `[bot]`, but does not appear to normalize an `app/` prefix.
- That can make `isTrustedIssueRecord()` reject a Sepo-created issue body marker, so `resolveChildIssueForTerminal()` returns null and `reportTerminalToParent()` silently does nothing.

This explains why #25 worked: #25 was an existing issue and Sepo added an adoption marker in a trusted comment. #135 was created by Sepo with the marker in the issue body, hitting the `app/...` author normalization edge case.

## Suggested fix

- Normalize GitHub App actor logins consistently in trust checks, e.g. treat `app/sepo-agent-app`, `sepo-agent-app[bot]`, and `sepo-agent-app` as the same trusted actor where appropriate.
- Alternatively, fetch issue authors through a source/API shape that returns the same bot login format used for comments.
- Add regression coverage for a Sepo-created child issue whose body contains a `sepo-sub-orchestrator` marker and whose issue author is reported as `app/sepo-agent-app`.
- Add diagnostics when `reportTerminalToParent()` cannot resolve or trust the child issue, so this failure mode does not silently stop parent orchestration.

## Acceptance criteria

- A Sepo-created child issue with a trusted body marker is accepted by `trustedSubOrchestrationIssue()`.
- A terminal child PR review verdict `SHIP` posts a parent progress report and dispatches/resumes the parent orchestrator.
- The child marker is updated from `running` to `done`.
- Tests cover the `app/<app-login>` author form.

comments.body:
Additional clarification/validation from the parent #126 incident:

The direct PR-level stop for PR #136 was normal: the final orchestrator run saw `SOURCE_ACTION=review` and `SOURCE_CONCLUSION=ship`, so the PR-level heuristic handoff correctly stopped with `review verdict is SHIP`. The bug is that this terminal child result was not reported back to parent #126, unlike the earlier child #25 / PR #134 path.

Why #134 handed back but #136 did not:

- #25 was an existing human-authored issue. Sepo adopted it as a child by adding a trusted marker comment. In `gh issue view --json comments`, Sepo comments are reported as author login `sepo-agent-app`, which matches the app-token viewer after normalizing `[bot]`.
- #135 was created by Sepo as a new sub-orchestrator child. Its marker is in the issue body, so the trust check uses the issue author from `gh issue view --json author`.
- For app-created issues, `gh issue view` consistently reports the author as `app/sepo-agent-app`, while the REST issue API reports the same creator as `sepo-agent-app[bot]`.

Validation examples:

```bash
$ gh issue view 127 --repo self-evolving/repo --json author --jq '.author.login'
app/sepo-agent-app
$ gh api repos/self-evolving/repo/issues/127 --jq '.user.login'
sepo-agent-app[bot]

$ gh issue view 132 --repo self-evolving/repo --json author --jq '.author.login'
app/sepo-agent-app
$ gh api repos/self-evolving/repo/issues/132 --jq '.user.login'
sepo-agent-app[bot]

$ gh issue view 135 --repo self-evolving/repo --json author --jq '.author.login'
app/sepo-agent-app
$ gh api repos/self-evolving/repo/issues/135 --jq '.user.login'
sepo-agent-app[bot]
```

This does not look random; it appears to be a consistent representation difference between `gh issue view`/GraphQL issue authors for GitHub App-created issues and REST/comment author logins.

So the suspected deeper bug is: `reportTerminalToParent()` can resolve the closing child issue from PR #136 (`Closes #135`), but `trustedSubOrchestrationIssue()` likely rejects #135's body marker because `normalizeActorLogin()` does not normalize `app/sepo-agent-app` to the same identity as `sepo-agent-app[bot]` / `sepo-agent-app`. That makes the parent handback silently no-op.

title: Fix sub-orchestrator parent resume for Sepo-created child issues
